contrarian_analysis.individual_investors_vs_smart_money: Dumb Money Confidence significantly exceeds Smart Money, a classic contrarian warning that retail optimism is elevated while institutional players remain cautious. This divergence typically precedes market corrections.
contrarian_analysis.overall_contrarian_reading: Mildly Bearish. Multiple indicators align: NAAIM near full allocation (96.67%), Equity PCR at extreme greed levels (0.43), Dumb Money > Smart Money, and credit spreads at historical complacency (16th percentile). The marginal buyer is exhausted.

federal_reserve.policy_stance: Federal Funds Rate at 3.75% (75bps cut over past 12 months). Market now pricing 45% probability of rate hikes in 2026, up from 1% a month ago.
federal_reserve.market_impact: Rising oil prices ($100+) and 10Y yield at 4.55% are reigniting inflation fears. The market is repricing Fed expectations from dovish to potentially hawkish, pressuring equity valuations especially in tech.

trump_admin.policy_actions: Trump-Xi meeting concluded without major deals. Nvidia chip sales were not discussed. Boeing received only 200 jet orders from China vs expected 500/ $77B.
trump_admin.market_impact: Geopolitical disappointment is weighing on trade-sensitive sectors. Industrial names underperforming. Uncertainty premium may rise if US-China détente stalls.

trade_policy.current_status: Global baseline tariff at 15% (raised from 10% in February). China-specific and sectoral tariffs (steel, autos, Section 301) remain in place.
trade_policy.market_impact: Trade policy uncertainty continues to pressure multinational industrials and manufacturers. The narrower-than-expected Boeing deal suggests Chinese retaliation risk persists.

policy_cross_impact.overall_policy_impact: Policy headwinds are intensifying. Fed repricing + tariff uncertainty + oil-driven inflation creates a tightening macro environment. This contrasts with the complacent risk pricing in credit markets (HY spreads at 2.76%). The policy-risk gap is a key vulnerability.

dashboard.contrarian_signal: Mildly Bearish (Dumb Money overheated + NAAIM fully loaded + PCR extreme)
dashboard.key_divergence: Indices near all-time highs but market breadth deteriorating (McClellan negative, Summation Index falling, IWM -2.41% while SPY -1.2%). Rally is narrowing to fewer names.
dashboard.market_breadth_status: Unhealthy. Small-caps significantly underperforming. NYSE McClellan Oscillator negative at -50.78. Advancing issues insufficient to support broad market strength.

tomorrow_watch.key_levels: SPY support 731-735 (May 12-13 consolidation), resistance 748-750. QQQ support 700-705, resistance 715-720. IWM critical support 275; break below could accelerate risk-off. VIX watch 20 level.
tomorrow_watch.catalyst_events: Monday: No major US macro data. Focus on Fed speaker commentary and oil price action. Q1 earnings season winding down but retail names still reporting. Bond market volatility remains elevated.
tomorrow_watch.risk_warnings: 30Y Treasury yields at 2007 highs are pressuring long-duration growth valuations. Credit spreads at 16th percentile offer minimal cushion if macro shocks hit. IWM underperformance is a leading indicator for broader risk sentiment deterioration.
